    Windows Setup: Restoring from renv.lock.win

    00_windows-renv-restore.Rmd

    Overview

    renv.lock.win is a Windows-compatible variant of the correlates_reporting2 lockfile. It differs from the original renv.lock in three ways:

    Change Detail
    Private SCHARP depot removed http://depot.pc.scharp.org/cran/4.4.2 removed from repositories
    doMC removed Unix-only (OS_type: unix) parallel backend; use doParallel instead
    RSPM URL updated cran/__linux__/noble/latest → cran/latest (cross-platform)

    A copy of the file is shipped with this package at inst/extdata/renv.lock.win.

    Prerequisites

    1. R 4.4.2 installed (the version recorded in the lockfile).
    2. renv installed:
    install.packages("renv")
    1. Windows-specific parallel packages (needed by the win/ script rewrites):
    install.packages(c("doParallel", "future", "future.apply", "taskscheduleR"))

    Restore steps

    1. Locate the lockfile

    The lockfile is shipped with utilrep2 at data/renv.lock.win. Find its path with:

    lockfile <- system.file("extdata", "renv.lock.win", package = "utilrep2")
lockfile

    Alternatively, if you have cloned correlates_reporting2 locally, use the copy there:

    lockfile <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2/renv.lock.win"

    2. Initialise renv in the project

    Navigate to the correlates_reporting2 root (or open it as a project in RStudio), then initialise renv without installing anything yet:

    setwd("/path/to/correlates_reporting2")
renv::init(bare = TRUE)

    3. Restore packages from the lockfile 

    renv::restore(lockfile = lockfile)

    renv will read the package versions, sources, and repositories from renv.lock.win and install everything into the project-local library. This step can take several minutes on first run.

    4. Verify the restore 

    renv::status(lockfile = lockfile)

    A clean restore shows no inconsistencies. If any packages could not be resolved from the public repositories (e.g. GitHub-sourced packages that require authentication), renv::status() will list them.

    GitHub-sourced packages

    Several packages in the lockfile are sourced from GitHub rather than CRAN:

    Package GitHub repo
    CFsurvival tedwestling/CFsurvival
    aucm youyifong/aucm
    diproperm youyifong/diproperm
    dummies cran/dummies
    kyotil youyifong/kyotil
    natmed2 benkeser/natmed2
    npthreshold jpspeng/npthreshold
    psbinary bblette1/psbinary
    sl3 tlverse/sl3
    survtmle benkeser/survtmle
    tmle3 tlverse/tmle3
    vaccine Avi-Kenny/vaccine

    If any of these require a GitHub PAT, set one before restoring:

    Sys.setenv(GITHUB_PAT = "your_pat_here")
renv::restore(lockfile = lockfile)

    Starting a session after restore

    Once packages are installed, configure utilrep2 with setup_session(). On Windows you will typically set platform = "win". If the win/ scripts live inside the project (the usual layout), no extra setting is needed:

    library(utilrep2)

setup_session(
  platform    = "win",
  data_path   = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2"
  # win_root defaults to <report_root>/win
)

    If the Windows-compatible scripts are stored separately — for example on a network share or a different local drive — supply win_root explicitly:

    setup_session(
  platform    = "win",
  data_path   = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2/data_clean",
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  report_root = "C:/projects/correlates_reporting2",
  win_root    = "D:/win_scripts"
)

    Call session_status() to confirm the configuration before running any analysis.

    Using doParallel instead of doMC

    doMC is not available on Windows. The win/ rewrites in this package replace all doMC-based parallelism with doParallel (PSOCK clusters):

    # Original Linux code (do not use on Windows):
# library(doMC); registerDoMC(cores = 4)

# Windows replacement (already in win/ scripts):
library(doParallel)
cl <- makeCluster(parallel::detectCores() - 1L)
registerDoParallel(cl)
on.exit(stopCluster(cl))

    See vignette("os-compatibility", package = "utilrep2") for a full audit of Linux-only patterns and their Windows replacements, and win/README.md in the package source for the complete list of rewritten scripts.

    See also

    • vignette("os-compatibility", package = "utilrep2")
    • vignette("getting-started", package = "utilrep2")
    • renv documentation
